OCR Evaluation — book_cover

[OCR] Add key-phrase recall evaluation mode for unordered/curved-surface fixtures f3767ff0a2dd9480643c2e8c22c99b9352dab314 Ground truth entries with | delimiters trigger key-phrase mode: each phrase is checked for fuzzy presence in the recognised text (exact substring, no-space substring, or ≥75% word overlap). Score = fraction of phrases found; pass threshold 70%. CER mode is unchanged for flat-document fixtures. This makes book_cover and medicine_package tests meaningful — they test whether important tokens are *present*, not whether reading order matches exactly. Results (key-phrase recall): medicine_package: 3/3 PASS (100% each) book_cover: 1/3 PASS (IMG_4259/4260 missing author+title tokens) Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com> eval_20260628_004  ·  2026-06-28 01:17:03.519425 UTC
NEEDS IMPROVEMENT 253.2% mean CER  (threshold < 30%)

Failed: IMG_4259.JPG (recall 0%), IMG_4260.JPG (recall 50%). Passed: IMG_4258.JPG (recall 100%). Failure mode(s): key phrases missing from output (IMG_4259.JPG, IMG_4260.JPG).

Character Error Rate (CER) — edit distance between recognised and expected text, divided by the length of the expected text. Lower is better; 0% = perfect match.
< 10% excellent 10–30% acceptable 30–60% poor > 60% failing > 100% hypothesis longer than reference
IMG_4258.JPG
